name: The Unicorn
artSubject: A radiant snow-white unicorn standing in a moonlit glade of silver ferns and glowing bluebells, its long spiralled horn shimmering with soft prismatic light. Its flowing mane and tail ripple like liquid pearl, catching drifting motes of stardust. Gentle luminous eyes, kind and wise, gaze warmly toward the viewer. Fireflies orbit its hooves and a delicate halo of pale violet light surrounds it, dew sparkling on nearby petals — utterly enchanting, serene and dreamlike. A small cluster of deep-purple violet flowers blooms in the grass at its hooves (a gentle nod to Violet).
theme: When you believe in magic, magic believes in you.
message: The unicorn steps softly out of the misty trees just for you, beloved, because your heart is pure enough to see what others have stopped believing in. When you trust that wonder is real, wonder draws closer — and the whole world begins to shimmer with the magic you feared you had outgrown.
book: The Unicorn appears when the world has told you to be sensible, to stop hoping for the shimmering and the impossible, and some tired part of you has almost agreed. Its deeper teaching is that belief is not a childish thing you leave behind; it is a doorway, and only those who keep it open ever walk through. We often think we must see the magic first and then believe, but the unicorn works the other way around — it comes to those who believe first, who keep a clean and trusting heart even without proof. Belief is what lets you see at all. So keep your wonder tender and unashamed. The magic you are longing for is longing for you too, and it steps nearer with every hope you refuse to give up.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns that clinging to a prettier fantasy can become a way of hiding from what is real, and that purity turned into judgement of others is no longer pure at all.
affirm: I keep my heart open to wonder, and wonder finds its way to me.

name: The Baby Unicorn
artSubject: An impossibly cute unicorn foal with soft downy cream fur and a tiny budding pearl horn no bigger than a rosebud, curled in a nest of pastel wildflowers and cotton-soft clouds. Its oversized dark eyes are tender and trusting, long lashes fluttering, tiny hooves tucked beneath it. A wisp of pink-gold mane curls over its brow, and gentle sparkles drift like fairy-snow around its ears. Dawn light glows warm and rosy, everything soft, safe and sweet. A soft pastel rainbow arcs gently across the dawn sky behind it.
theme: Every gentle new beginning carries its own quiet magic.
message: On wobbly new legs the baby unicorn takes its very first steps, beloved, and it is not ashamed of being small or slow. Something in your life is just beginning too, still tender and unsteady — be gentle with it, and with yourself, for the softest starts so often grow the brightest magic.
book: The Baby Unicorn arrives at the fragile start of something — a new hope, a new skill, a new season of your heart — when you feel too small or too clumsy to deserve it yet. Its deeper teaching is that everything strong was once brand new, and newness is not a weakness to hurry past but a holiness to protect. We so often rush our beginnings, embarrassed by how little we can do, comparing our first wobbling steps to someone else's full gallop. But no creature is asked to be grown the moment it is born. Let your beginning simply be a beginning. Give it warmth, patience, and time, and the very innocence that feels so vulnerable now will become the source of your gentlest and most lasting strength.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns against despising your own beginnings for being small, or rushing a tender new thing into growing up before it has had the chance to just be new.
affirm: I am gentle with all that is new in me, and I let it grow in its own good time.

name: The Pegasus
artSubject: A magnificent winged horse soaring through a vast dawn sky, great feathered wings outstretched and edged with gold where the sunrise touches them. Its silver-white coat gleams, mane billowing in the high clean wind, powerful yet graceful. Below drift soft rose-and-amber clouds; above, the first stars still glimmer. Its noble face is calm and free, eyes bright with wonder as it climbs ever higher — a scene of soaring hope and boundless open sky.
theme: Spread your wings and rise gently above every fear.
message: The pegasus spreads its great white wings and lifts into the open sky, beloved, showing you that the thing you fear looks far smaller from above. You were never meant to stay earthbound and afraid. Rise gently — not by fighting your fear, but by remembering you have always had wings.
book: The Pegasus arrives when something has kept you small and grounded — a worry, a doubt, a cage you have half-forgotten is made only of fear. Its deeper teaching is that freedom is not the absence of fear but the courage to rise in spite of it, lifting up until what once loomed so large becomes just one small patch of ground below. We often think we must destroy our fears before we can be free, and so we wrestle with them forever and never leave the earth. But the pegasus does not fight the ground; it simply opens its wings and climbs. Your courage need not be loud or violent — it is the quiet decision to lift your gaze and trust the air. Spread what you have kept folded. You are freer than you have let yourself believe.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns against using flight as escape — soaring away from what still needs tending on the ground — or mistaking recklessness for the true, gentle courage that rises with care.
affirm: I spread my wings and rise gently above my fear.

name: The Phoenix
artSubject: A glorious phoenix rising with wings sweeping wide, its plumage a cascade of living flame — crimson, molten gold and ember-orange feathers glowing like sunrise, each tip trailing sparks that turn into tiny stars. A crest of radiant golden feathers crowns its head, and its bright warm eyes shine with courage, not menace. Soft luminous embers spiral upward around it against a deep twilight sky, warm light bathing everything — triumphant, magnificent and full of hope.
theme: Every ending is the doorway to a brilliant new beginning.
message: Something in your life has turned to ash, beloved, and you fear that is the end of the story — but the phoenix knows the oldest secret of all: ash is exactly where new wings are made. What feels like an ending is a beginning you cannot see yet.
book: The Phoenix arrives when a chapter has ended and you are standing in the smoke, sure nothing good could rise from so much loss. Its deeper teaching is that endings and beginnings are one doorway seen from two sides — the phoenix does not fear the fire because it knows it will rise from it, brighter than before. We so often clutch what is already gone, mistaking the burning for the tragedy when the burning is really the making. Let what is finished be finished, gently. Something luminous in you is gathering itself, getting ready to rise — and rise it will.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns against rushing your rebirth before the ashes have cooled, or clutching the old form so tightly the new one cannot take shape.
affirm: I rise again and again, more truly myself each time.

name: The Dragon
artSubject: A noble, friendly dragon with smooth emerald-and-jade scales that shimmer like gemstones, curled protectively in a sunlit mountain meadow. Its large soulful eyes are warm amber and kind, a soft smile at the corner of its mouth, gentle curved horns and small folded wings. Butterflies rest fearlessly on its snout and a little glow of warm light drifts from its nostrils like friendly steam. Wildflowers bloom around its resting claws — majestic yet utterly gentle and approachable.
theme: True strength is the kind that stays gentle.
message: The great dragon could burn the whole forest, beloved, and instead it warms one cold traveller and keeps watch over the small and the sleeping. That is the secret of real power: the stronger you truly are, the gentler you can afford to be. Your kindness is not weakness — it is strength that has grown wise.
book: The Dragon arrives when you are learning what to do with your own power — your voice, your fire, your ability to affect the people around you — and wondering whether being strong means being hard. Its deeper teaching is that the mightiest creatures have the least need to prove it; true nobility is strength placed in service of the gentle and the small. We often confuse power with force, believing we must roar and dominate to be respected, when in truth that is the fear of the weak wearing a fierce mask. The dragon has nothing to prove. It knows exactly how strong it is, and so it chooses tenderness, protection, and honour. Let your strength grow kind. The fire in you is real — use it to warm, to guard, and to lift, and you will be noble in the oldest and truest sense.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns that unwatched strength can curdle into intimidation or hoarding — power used to guard only itself — and reminds you that force without kindness is not nobility but fear in disguise.
affirm: My strength is real, and I keep it gentle.

name: The Gnome
artSubject: A cheerful little gnome with a long silvery beard and a tall pointed red cap, kneeling in a cosy woodland garden tending tiny glowing mushrooms and sprouting seedlings. His round rosy cheeks and twinkling kind eyes beam with quiet joy, his clearly visible friendly face wrinkled from smiling. Earth-brown tunic, sturdy little boots, a wooden watering can beside him. Warm dappled sunlight filters through mossy roots and toadstools, dewdrops glinting — snug, homely and full of earthy warmth.
theme: Stay grounded, tend small things, and be content.
message: The gnome moves slowly through his garden, beloved, tending each root and stone as though it were the whole world — because to him, it is. He has learned the secret the hurried never find: a small life, fully loved, is not small at all, and contentment grows in the soil right beneath your feet.
book: The Gnome arrives when you have been reaching for somewhere else, someone else, some bigger and shinier life, and have stopped seeing the quiet riches already around you. His deeper teaching is that wisdom does not live on far-off mountaintops but in the patient tending of ordinary things — a swept floor, a watered plant, a kept promise. We often mistake contentment for settling, as though being at peace with our lives means we have given up on them; but the gnome knows that to love what is here is the beginning of every real abundance. Slow down. Put your hands in the earth of your own days. The ground you already stand on is holy, and it has been waiting for you to notice.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns that contentment can curdle into stubbornness or a fear of ever leaving your burrow — comfort was meant to root you, not to bury you.
affirm: I find deep contentment in tending the small, good things of my life.

name: The Pixie
artSubject: A tiny impish pixie mid-giggle, darting through a sunbeam on gossamer dragonfly wings that flash turquoise and gold. Pointed ears, a mop of tousled leafy hair, upturned nose and a mischievous grin light up her clearly visible sweet round face. She wears a little tunic of overlapping petals and trails a comet-tail of glittering pixie dust as she spins. Bluebells sway and ladybirds scatter playfully around her — sprightly, joyful and full of happy mischief.
theme: Let your heart stay light and playful.
message: The pixie darts by on a giggle and a gust of wind, beloved, here to remind you that you were not made only to be serious. Life has grown heavy and over-careful lately — she invites you to laugh again, to play, and to let your heart go light as a seed on the breeze.
book: The Pixie flits into your reading when life has become all duty and no delight, when you have forgotten that joy is not a reward for finishing your work but a way of doing it. Her deeper teaching is that lightness is its own kind of wisdom — a playful heart bends where a solemn one would break, and finds doorways where a worried mind sees only walls. We tend to think seriousness makes us good and mischief makes us foolish, but the pixie knows a giggle can heal what a lecture never could. Let yourself be silly. Chase something just for the fun of it. Trust that the same lightness that makes you laugh also makes you strong, and that a heart at play is a heart that remembers it is alive.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns that play can tip into mischief that stings, or a lightness used to dodge the feelings that ask, gently, to be felt.
affirm: I let my heart stay light, playful, and free.

name: The Fairy
artSubject: A graceful fairy hovering beside a dew-jewelled flower at twilight, delicate iridescent wings catching soft lavender and rose light. Her clearly visible gentle face is serene and lovely, framed by flowing hair woven with tiny blossoms, eyes sparkling with wonder. She cups a glowing wish-light in her palms as luminous pollen and fireflies drift around her. Her gown is spun from petals and moonlight, and everything glimmers with a quiet everyday enchantment — tender, luminous and full of gentle wonder.
theme: There is magic hidden in the smallest everyday things.
message: The fairy leans close and whispers what she has always known, beloved: the world is far more enchanted than it lets on. Dew on a web, a wish on a candle, the hush before dawn — magic is not hiding in some far kingdom, but tucked inside the smallest ordinary moments, waiting for you to notice.
book: The Fairy appears when your wonder has gone quiet — when days blur past unnoticed and you have begun to believe the world is only what it seems. Her deeper teaching is that magic was never somewhere else; it lives in your capacity to truly see, so that the same garden can be dull to a tired eye and dazzling to a delighted one. We often wait for something grand and unmistakable to prove that life is enchanted, and so we miss the wishes and wonders offered to us daily. The fairy asks you to slow down and look again. Make a wish and mean it. Notice the light. The magic you have been longing for is quietly present in the life you already have, and it grows brighter the moment you believe in it.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns against chasing endless enchantment to escape the real, or dismissing wonder as childish when it is, in truth, the wisest way to see.
affirm: I notice the quiet magic woven through my everyday world.

name: The Halfling
artSubject: A cosy, curly-haired halfling with rosy cheeks and big kind eyes standing contentedly at the round green door of a snug hillside burrow, warm lantern light spilling from within. His clearly visible cheerful face beams with easy warmth; he wears a soft waistcoat and holds a mug of steaming cocoa, bare furry-topped feet on the garden path. Runner beans, pumpkins and marigolds fill the little garden, woodsmoke curling from a round chimney at golden hour — homely, heartwarming and full of comfort.
theme: Simple joys hold a quiet, surprising courage.
message: The halfling loves a warm hearth, a full plate, and good friends, beloved — and yet when the world asks something braver of him, he goes anyway. His gentle secret is that those who most treasure comfort often carry the deepest courage, for they know exactly what they are protecting and why it is worth the journey.
book: The Halfling arrives to honor the quiet, homely joys you may have been taught to think of as small — the meal shared, the door left open, the simple pleasure of an ordinary day. His deeper teaching is that these comforts are not the opposite of courage but its very root: it is love of home that gives a gentle heart something worth being brave for. We often imagine heroes as fearless giants and overlook the soft-footed ones who tremble and step forward anyway. The halfling shows that the bravest thing is often done by someone who would much rather be safe at home, and does the hard thing for love. Cherish your simple joys without apology. They are not a retreat from a courageous life — they are what makes one possible.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns that a love of comfort can quietly become a cage, keeping you so safe at home that the braver, truer road is never taken.
affirm: My love of simple joys gives me a quiet, steady courage.

name: The Mermaid
artSubject: A beautiful mermaid resting on a smooth moonstone rock in a glowing turquoise lagoon, her shimmering scaled tail in shades of aqua and pearl trailing into the clear water. Her clearly visible kind and lovely face is calm and gentle, long flowing hair adorned with pearls and tiny starfish. Bioluminescent jellyfish drift softly around her and gentle waves catch the silver moonlight. She holds a glowing pearl to her heart, expression tender and wise — serene, dreamy and full of deep feeling.
theme: Trust the deep, feeling wisdom of your heart.
message: The mermaid rises from waters no eye can measure, beloved, and beckons you to trust the deep feeling moving through you. Your emotions are not a storm to be silenced but a tide that carries real wisdom — dive beneath the surface of your busy thoughts, and let your heart tell you what it has always known.
book: The Mermaid surfaces when you have been living too much in your head, weighing and reasoning while a quieter, deeper knowing goes unheard. Her teaching is that feeling is a form of intelligence all its own — the body and the heart perceive truths the thinking mind arrives at far too late, if at all. We are often taught to distrust our emotions, to call them irrational and push them under; but the mermaid knows the deep is not dangerous, only unfamiliar to those who never learned to swim in it. Let yourself feel what you feel, fully and without shame. Beneath the waves of worry there is a still, wise current, and it has been trying to guide you home. Trust it. Your heart has depths that know the way.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns that feeling can pull you under when you mistake every passing wave for truth, or let it drown out the steadying voice of the shore.
affirm: I trust the deep, feeling wisdom of my heart.

name: The Goblin
artSubject: A cheeky, good-hearted little goblin with knobbly green skin, big pointed ears and an enormous friendly grin showing one crooked tooth, his clearly visible face bright with warmth and clever twinkling eyes. He wears a patchwork jerkin stitched from many colours and proudly holds up a shiny found trinket he wants to share. He crouches in a cosy burrow-workshop of gears, buttons and glowing lanterns — playful, endearing and clearly kind despite his rascally looks.
theme: Never judge by appearances — cleverness and kindness wear many faces.
message: The goblin knows exactly what you think when you see him, beloved — and he is far more than that first glance suggests. Behind his crooked grin lives a quick mind and a surprisingly tender heart, here to remind you that kindness and cleverness often arrive in unexpected shapes, and the truest treasures rarely look the part.
book: The Goblin scurries into your reading when you have been judging too quickly — a person, a situation, perhaps even yourself — by the surface alone. His deeper teaching is that appearances are the least reliable map to the heart of things: the plain-looking stranger, the awkward moment, the option no one else wanted may hold exactly the cleverness or kindness you have been missing. We so often trust the shiny and dismiss the odd, and in doing so we overlook real gifts wearing humble clothes. The goblin invites you to look twice and listen longer. Give the overlooked a fair chance, including the overlooked parts of yourself. Wit and warmth wear a thousand disguises, and life delights in surprising those humble enough to be surprised.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns that cleverness untethered from heart becomes mere trickery, and that quick judgments cut both ways — do not let others' first glances define you, either.
affirm: I look past appearances to the cleverness and kindness within.

name: The Wizard
artSubject: A wise old wizard with a long flowing white beard and a star-strewn deep-blue robe, standing in a candlelit tower study among floating books and glowing scrolls. His clearly visible face is kind and serene, crinkled with a gentle knowing smile, eyes warm beneath bushy brows. He raises a staff topped with a softly glowing crystal, conjuring a swirl of golden constellations in the air. Astrolabes, potion jars and a curious owl surround him — magisterial, warm and full of gentle wisdom.
theme: Trust the wisdom you have patiently learned.
message: You have gathered more wisdom than you know, beloved — every lesson, every quiet year of practice lives inside you now like a spell you learned long ago by heart. The Wizard smiles and places your own staff in your hand: the moment has come to trust what you know and use it.
book: The Wizard steps into your life when you stand at the edge of something important, whispering that you are not ready, that someone wiser should surely go in your place. His deeper teaching is that mastery was never a lightning bolt — it is the patient gathering of a thousand small lessons, until one day you turn around and find you have become the very person you were waiting for. We often mistake wisdom for something that lives only in other people's books and voices, and so we keep searching outside for what already hums within. The Wizard lifts his staff and shows you the truth: the knowing is yours, earned honestly, sturdy and real. Trust it. Speak it. Let your patient learning become the magic it was always meant to be.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns of hoarding knowledge without ever daring to use it, or hiding behind endless study because trusting your own wisdom feels riskier than quietly doubting it.
affirm: I trust the wisdom I have patiently earned, and I use it.

name: The Witch
artSubject: A kind, wise witch in a cosy herb-strewn cottage kitchen, gently stirring a softly glowing cauldron of starlight. Her clearly visible face is warm and lovely with a gentle smile and bright caring eyes, a soft pointed hat tilted over waves of hair braided with dried lavender. Bundles of herbs, jars of honey and hanging flowers surround her, a friendly black cat curled at her feet and a moth on her sleeve. Warm candlelight and drifting sparkles fill the air — welcoming, gentle and never frightening.
theme: Follow your intuition and the natural magic within you.
message: There is a knowing in you older than words, beloved — a quiet tug in your belly that points true long before your mind can explain it. The Witch teaches you to trust that whisper, to gather what nature offers and weave your own small magic; your intuition was never wrong, only unlistened to.
book: The Witch arrives when you have been talking yourself out of what you already feel to be true, choosing the loud advice of others over the soft certainty rising within. Her deeper teaching is that intuition is not superstition but a wild and natural intelligence — the same wisdom that tells birds when to fly and roots which way to grow lives in you too. People often imagine magic as something rare and far away, when really it is the everyday craft of paying close attention: to the moon, to your own body, to the quiet yes and no that rise unbidden. The Witch invites you back to that listening. Trust the feeling before the reasons arrive. Tend your instincts like a garden, and watch what naturally, beautifully grows.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns of drowning out your own inner voice with other people's certainty, or mistaking fear's loud alarm for the calm, quiet knowing that is true intuition.
affirm: I trust the natural magic and quiet knowing within me.

name: King Neptune
artSubject: A mighty, kindly sea-king enthroned on a great coral throne beneath sunlit waves, his clearly visible noble face framed by a flowing sea-green beard and a crown of pearl and gold. Broad-shouldered and serene, he holds a glowing trident that calms the currents around him. Dolphins, sea-turtles and shimmering fish gather peacefully at his side; rays of golden light pierce the aquamarine deep, and gentle bubbles rise. His expression is strong, steady and reassuring — a powerful yet protective guardian of the calm sea. A beautiful mermaid with a clear, lovely face rests gracefully at his side.
theme: Rule your inner tides with steady, protective calm.
message: Your feelings move like the great ocean, beloved — vast, deep, sometimes stormy — yet you are not the storm; you are the king who rules the whole sea from a place of calm. King Neptune shows you that true power is not shouting over the waves, but growing quiet and steady enough to hold them all.
book: King Neptune rises when your emotions have grown wild and you fear they might sweep you away — anger like a riptide, sorrow like a sudden flood. His deeper teaching is that a great sea is not weak for having storms; its power lies in its depth, in the still and unshaken calm that lives far beneath the surface waves. We often think being strong means never feeling the tempest, and so we clamp down hard, or let the waves rule us instead. Neptune lifts his trident gently and shows another way: feel the tides fully, yet remain the sovereign of your own waters. Protect what is precious to you with steady calm rather than fury. When you rule your inner ocean from that deep, quiet throne, no storm can ever overthrow you.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns of letting stormy feelings seize the throne and rule you, or of freezing the sea into cold, hard control that mistakes numbness for calm.
affirm: I rule my inner tides with steady, protective calm.

name: The Cherub
artSubject: A sweet round-cheeked cherub with tiny fluffy feathered wings, floating on a soft rosy cloud in a warm golden sky. Its clearly visible cherubic face is angelic and gentle, curls of golden hair, big loving eyes and a serene little smile. It cradles a glowing heart-shaped light in its small hands, and a soft halo of pink-gold radiance surrounds it. Delicate rose petals and drifting feathers float nearby in the tender dawn light — pure, loving and heart-meltingly gentle.
theme: You are loved, tender and utterly cherished.
message: Before you did a single thing to earn it, beloved, you were already loved — completely, tenderly, exactly as you are. The Cherub flutters close to remind you of the softest truth of all: you are cherished beyond measure, held in a love that asks nothing of you but to let yourself be held.
book: The Cherub appears when you have been trying so hard to deserve love — being good enough, useful enough, perfect enough — that you have forgotten love was never a prize to be won. Its deeper teaching is tender and simple: you are cherished already, wholly and freely, the way a small child is adored simply for being. We so often armor our hearts, believing we must protect ourselves or prove our worth before we are safe enough to be loved. The Cherub slips right past that armor with a giggle. It reminds you that tenderness is not weakness, that letting yourself be loved is its own quiet courage. You do not have to earn a single drop of it. Soften, beloved. You are, and have always been, utterly cherished.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns of believing love must be earned or performed, or building such walls around a tender heart that the very love longing to reach you cannot get in.
affirm: I am loved, tender and utterly cherished, exactly as I am.

name: The Star Being
artSubject: A luminous celestial being formed of gentle starlight and drifting galaxies, its clearly visible serene and beautiful face glowing softly like the dawn, eyes deep and kind as the night sky. Constellations trace across its skin and flowing hair of shimmering nebula-light streams with tiny stars. It stands cradling a small glowing planet in its palms, arms open in welcome, surrounded by swirling cosmic dust in violet, indigo and gold. Vast, peaceful and awe-inspiring — a tender embodiment of cosmic belonging.
theme: You are made of stars and belong to something vast.
message: The atoms in your bones were forged in ancient stars, beloved — you are not a small speck lost in the dark, but the cosmos itself grown able to wonder and to love. The Star Being drifts near to remind you: you belong to something vast and luminous, and it has never once let you go.
book: The Star Being arrives on the nights you feel most alone — small, unseen, adrift in a universe too big to notice you. Its deeper teaching turns that fear gently inside out: the same stardust that lit the galaxies is the very stuff your own heart is made of, so you are not separate from the cosmos but woven right into it. We often imagine belonging as something we must find out there, in the right group or the right place, when the truth is we were never once cast out. The Star Being points to the night sky and then to your chest, showing they shine with the same ancient light. You are connected to all of it — the stars, the earth, every other soul. Lonely as you may feel, you are held in a vast and endless belonging.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns of feeling so small and disconnected that you forget your place in the whole, or of drifting off into the cosmos to escape the tender work of belonging here on earth.
affirm: I am made of stars and I belong to something vast.

name: The Leprechaun
artSubject: A cheerful young leprechaun with rosy cheeks, twinkling green eyes and a warm open smile, seated cross-legged on a mossy emerald hill. He wears a tidy green coat with polished gold buttons and a buckled hat tipped back off his brow. In his cupped hands a small pot brims with softly glowing gold coins, and a luminous rainbow arcs down to meet him. Dewdrop clovers sparkle around his boots, tiny golden motes drift in sunlit air, and a gentle grin lights his clearly visible, friendly face.
theme: The real treasure waits at the end of your own rainbow, so chase it with a playful heart.
message: There is gold at the end of your rainbow, beloved — but the Leprechaun laughs and shares a secret: the merry chasing was the treasure all along. Follow your own delight with a light and playful heart, for luck loves most to visit those who go looking with a grin.
book: The Leprechaun comes skipping into your life when everything has grown too heavy and serious, and you have forgotten that joy itself is a kind of fortune. His deeper teaching, told with a wink, is that the pot of gold is real — but it waits at the end of your own rainbow, not someone else's, and the truest riches are gathered in the playful skipping toward it. People often chase luck grimly, believing treasure is only the shiny thing at the finish line, and so they miss all the gold scattered along the path. The Leprechaun rattles his coins and dares you to lighten up. Chase what makes your heart leap. Trust that a playful spirit draws good fortune the way a smile draws a smile. The luckiest treasure of all is a life delighted in.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns of chasing someone else's rainbow instead of your own, or grasping so greedily at the gold that you trample the very playfulness that makes luck want to find you.
affirm: I chase my own rainbow with a playful, delighted heart.

name: The Troll
artSubject: A huge, kindly troll with a broad calm face, soft mossy-green skin, gentle deep-set eyes and a shy, warm smile, sitting peacefully beneath a stone bridge dappled with sunlight. Little ferns and wildflowers sprout from his shoulders, and a tiny bluebird rests trustingly in his enormous open palm. Smooth river stones glow around him, soft golden light filters through leaves overhead, and everything about his patient posture radiates gentleness. His friendly face is clearly visible, more grandfatherly than fearsome.
theme: You are gentle and strong, and no harsh word can define who you truly are.
message: People may have called you slow, or heavy, or strange, beloved, but the troll only smiles, for it has stood like a mountain long after the shouting faded. You are gentle and strong at once, and no harsh word spoken over you can ever say who you truly are.
book: The Troll lumbers into your reading when you have been misjudged — perhaps called clumsy or too much, or made to feel that your quiet, steady way is somehow wrong. Its deeper teaching is that true strength is rarely loud; the troll moves slowly because it is carrying something real, and it does not crumble when unkind words are thrown its way. We often mistake gentleness for weakness and patience for having nothing to say, when in truth the steadiest heart is the strongest one in the room. Do not shrink yourself to fit another's small idea of you. Stand where you stand, kind and unhurried and unmoved. The names others give you wash off like rain from stone — underneath, you remain exactly, unshakably yourself.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns that steadiness can harden into stubbornness, or that patience with unkindness becomes a wall you hide behind rather than a strength you stand on.
affirm: I am gentle and strong, and no harsh word can define me.

name: The Elf
artSubject: A graceful woodland elf with fair, luminous skin, gentle almond eyes, gracefully pointed ears and a calm, kind, clearly visible face framed by flowing chestnut hair woven with leaves. She wears soft mossy-green and gold robes and stands amid ancient sunlit trees, one hand resting on glowing bark. Fireflies and floating pollen shimmer in shafts of golden forest light, silver ferns curl at her feet, and her serene knowing smile radiates quiet forest wisdom.
theme: Trust your keen sight and the quiet wisdom the woodland has always known.
message: The elf sees what others walk right past, beloved — the truth glimmering just beneath the surface, the small sign the forest leaves for those who slow down enough to notice. Trust your keen sight now, and the quiet knowing that has lived in you all along; it has never once led you wrong.
book: The Elf steps softly into your reading when you have been doubting what you already sense to be true. Its deeper teaching is that real wisdom is quiet — it does not shout to be believed; it simply waits, patient as an old tree, for you to trust it. Elves see clearly because they are unhurried and unafraid, watching the world with gentle, ancient eyes. We often talk ourselves out of what we perceive, calling our clearest instincts foolish because no one else has spoken them aloud. But your sight is sharper than you have been taught to admit. Honour the small, certain knowing in your chest. The woodland has kept its wisdom for a thousand years by listening closely, and you are far wiser than you think the moment you finally listen too.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns that keen sight can curdle into aloof judgment, watching everything from a cool distance while forgetting to step in and truly live.
affirm: I trust my clear sight and the quiet wisdom within me.

name: The Alchemist
artSubject: A warm, kindly alchemist at work in his candlelit study — a man in the prime of life with neat BLACK hair, gentle warm BROWN eyes, and short, neatly-trimmed facial hair (light stubble or a short beard, NOT long or full). He holds a small glowing vial of golden light with quiet wonder, surrounded by leather-bound books, brass instruments, star charts, bubbling flasks and drifting motes of gold; a sleepy cat curls on the desk. Deep jewel tones, candle-warm light, intricate, magical and inviting.
theme: With patience and quiet magic you turn the ordinary into gold and dreams into reality.
message: The alchemist bends over the workbench late into the night, beloved, patiently turning plain metal into gold — not by wishing, but by tending the fire and trusting the slow magic of the work. What you are building will become something luminous; keep going, gently, and let your dreams take shape in your hands.
book: The Alchemist arrives in your reading when you are in the middle of making something — a dream, a change, a new version of your life — and cannot yet see the gold it will become. Its deeper teaching is that transformation is a craft, not an accident: it asks for patience, for tending, for the willingness to keep working while the results are still hidden. We often mistake magic for something instant, and so we quit just before the ordinary turns to gold. But true alchemy is slow and faithful. Trust the quiet work of your hands and heart. Keep stirring, keep shaping, keep showing up to the fire. What looks like plain, unremarkable effort today is the very thing that becomes treasure — dreams made real, one patient day at a time.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns against chasing quick transformation or forcing the result before its time, mistaking impatience for vision and abandoning the work just before it turns to gold.
affirm: With patience and quiet magic, I turn my dreams into gold.

name: The Goddess
artSubject: A radiant goddess of the sacred feminine with a serene, beautiful, clearly visible face and warm compassionate eyes, standing crowned in soft starlight. She wears flowing amethyst and rose-gold robes, a delicate crescent-moon diadem and glowing constellations across her shoulders. One hand rests over her heart, the other open in blessing, releasing gentle petals of light. A luminous halo of dawn colours surrounds her, and her calm, self-assured smile radiates dignity, wholeness and quiet strength.
theme: You are already whole and worthy, and your quiet power is sacred.
message: The Goddess does not become worthy, beloved — she simply is, and she has been since the very beginning, needing no one's permission to shine. You are already whole exactly as you are; the power rising softly in you is not borrowed or earned, it is sacred, and it is entirely yours.
book: The Goddess rises into your reading when you have been feeling small — as though you must prove your worth, earn your place, or fix yourself before you are allowed to be loved. Her deeper teaching is that wholeness is not a reward for good behaviour; it is your nature, present in you now, complete before you do a single thing. We are taught to chase worthiness as if it lives outside us, in others' approval or some far-off future self, and so we miss the sacred power already glowing quietly within. You do not need to be more to be enough. Stand tall in your own gentle magnificence. The Goddess in you has always been whole, always been worthy — and the moment you remember this, your quiet power becomes a light no one can dim.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns that forgotten worth can turn into endless seeking for outside approval, or that power misused hardens into pride that forgets everyone else is sacred too.
affirm: I am already whole and worthy, and my quiet power is sacred.

name: The Serpent
artSubject: A wise, gentle serpent with soft emerald-and-gold scales that shimmer like polished jewels, coiled peacefully around a blossoming branch. Its kind, gentle eyes glow with warm amber light and its face is calm and friendly, not fearful. A translucent, iridescent shed skin drifts away like ribbon beside it, revealing bright new scales beneath. Healing green leaves and glowing white flowers unfurl around its coils, soft golden light haloes the scene, and everything radiates gentle rebirth and quiet wisdom.
theme: Shed what no longer serves you and let gentle renewal heal and make you new.
message: The serpent slips free of its old skin without fear or sorrow, beloved, knowing that what it sheds was never meant to be carried forever. Something in you is ready to be released now — an old story, an old ache — and beneath it waits skin that is new, tender, and beautifully alive.
book: The Serpent glides into your reading when you have outgrown something you are still holding — a habit, a hurt, a version of yourself that no longer fits. Its deeper teaching is that shedding is not loss but healing: the serpent does not mourn the skin it leaves behind, because leaving it is exactly how it grows. We often cling to the old skin long past its time, afraid that releasing it means losing part of who we are, when in truth what we release was only ever a covering, not the living thing beneath. Let it go, gently. There is no need to tear yourself free in a rush. Renewal comes softly to those who trust it, and the tender new self emerging in you now is closer to your truth than anything you leave behind.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns against clinging to an old skin out of fear, or tearing it away too harshly before the new one has grown strong enough to protect you.
affirm: I release what no longer serves me and welcome gentle renewal.

name: The Tree Spirit
artSubject: A gentle tree spirit whose kind, serene face emerges softly from the bark of a great ancient tree, with warm glowing eyes and a peaceful, clearly visible smile framed by leafy hair and blossom. Luminous roots spread deep into rich glowing earth while golden light streams through a broad emerald canopy. Tiny songbirds nest in her branches, fireflies drift among the leaves, and soft moss and wildflowers glow at her base. Everything radiates patience, steadiness and living warmth.
theme: Grow steady and tall, for deep roots and patience make you unshakeable.
message: The tallest tree in the forest never hurried, beloved — it simply kept growing, one quiet ring at a time, sending its roots deeper each season it stood. You are growing too, slowly and surely, and the patience you are practising now is exactly what will one day make you impossible to knock down.
book: The Tree Spirit arrives when you are weary of waiting, certain that everyone else is blooming faster while you stand still. Its deeper teaching is that real strength is built underground, in the slow, unseen work of roots — the part of you no storm can reach. We so often measure our worth by how tall and quickly we rise, forgetting that a tree grows down as much as it grows up, and that this hidden growth is what holds it steady when the winds finally come. Do not rush your becoming. Keep your feet in the earth and your face toward the light, and trust the quiet seasons as much as the bright ones. What is growing in you cannot be hurried, but it can be trusted — and it is growing tall.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns of an impatience that keeps yanking up its own roots to check if they are growing, or a rigidity that mistakes never bending for real strength.
affirm: I grow deep and steady, and nothing can shake what is truly rooted in me.

name: The Jasmine Bloom
artSubject: A luminous night-blooming jasmine vine unfurling star-shaped white blossoms that glow softly against a deep twilight-blue sky. Among the petals, tiny winged pixies with sweet, clearly visible smiling faces and gossamer wings dance and swirl, trailing sparkles of silver light. Each little pixie glows gently, sprinkling stardust over the flowers. A soft crescent moon hangs above, dewdrops shimmer like tiny jewels on the leaves, and the whole scene radiates delicate, fragrant, magical sweetness.
theme: Bloom beautifully even in the dark, and your sweetness will light the night.
message: The jasmine keeps its sweetest gift for the dark, beloved, waiting until the sun has gone to open its little white stars and pour perfume into the night. You need not wait for perfect conditions to blossom — even here, even now, in whatever shadow you find yourself, you are allowed to bloom and share your sweetness.
book: The Jasmine Bloom arrives when you have been holding your beauty back, waiting for a brighter, safer, more perfect moment to let yourself shine. Its deeper teaching is that grace does not depend on golden conditions — the jasmine blooms most fragrantly at night, offering its sweetness precisely when the world seems darkest. We often believe we must be fully healed, ready, or unafraid before we can open, mistaking hardship for a closed door. But your gentleness is needed now, not later, and a small kindness offered in a dark hour carries further than a grand one in the sun. Do not hide your softness or apologise for your sweetness. Open, quietly and unhurried, and let who you are perfume the whole room. Your blooming was never meant to wait for permission.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns of hiding your sweetness until conditions feel flawless, or pouring out so much grace for others that your own bloom goes untended and unwatered.
affirm: I bloom where I am planted, and my sweetness lights even the darkest night.

name: The Griffin
artSubject: A magnificent, noble griffin with the golden feathered head of a great eagle, warm intelligent amber eyes and a gentle, majestic expression, its tawny lion body strong and calm. It stands protectively atop a sunlit cliff, vast golden-bronze wings spread in a sheltering arc over a small nest of glowing eggs. Warm dawn light gilds every feather, soft clouds drift below, and its watchful, kindly gaze radiates loyalty and steadfast guardianship rather than menace.
theme: You are watched over and protected by a loyal, loving guardian.
message: With the far-seeing eyes of an eagle and the brave heart of a lion, the griffin keeps fierce watch over what it loves, beloved — and right now it is watching over you. You are not walking alone or unguarded; something loyal and strong stands close at your side, and you can rest inside that protection tonight.
book: The Griffin arrives when you feel exposed, unguarded, or as though you must face everything entirely on your own. Its deeper teaching is that you are held by a loyalty fiercer than you know — part eagle, part lion, the griffin sees far and guards close, and it has quietly chosen you. We often believe that being protected means never facing anything hard, and grow frightened the moment trouble comes, sure we have been abandoned. But a guardian does not remove every storm; it stands beside you within the storm so that you are never alone in it. Let yourself feel watched over. Lean back into the strength that surrounds you, trust the loyal presence at your shoulder, and set down the exhausting guard you have carried by yourself for so long. You are safe, you are seen, and you are dearly protected.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns of becoming so guarded that you push away the very love that would protect you, or of forgetting that even the strongest guardian cannot shield you from every lesson you are meant to grow through.
affirm: I am watched over, protected, and never truly alone.

name: The Raven
theme: A message is near, and something wise is watching over you.
artSubject: Two glossy black ravens with a beautiful iridescent blue-and-purple sheen on their feathers in an enchanted twilight wood — one perched close and bold on a mossy branch, regarding the viewer with a bright, warm, intelligent eye; the other a little further back, softer and more shy. Soft golden light and drifting motes, a scatter of stars and a slim crescent moon behind them, gentle mist. Wise, magical, watchful and friendly — never spooky.
message: The raven does not appear by accident, beloved — it comes as a messenger, bright-eyed and clever, to tell you that you are watched over far more closely than you know. Pay attention to the small signs today; something wise and loving is trying, gently, to get a message through to you.
book: The Raven arrives when you have been feeling unseen, or unsure whether the quiet signs around you mean anything at all. Its deeper teaching is that the world is more alive and attentive than it looks — messengers move between the seen and the unseen, and you are accompanied by a wise, loving presence that never truly leaves your side. We so often dismiss the nudges, the repeated symbols, the sense of being watched over, calling them coincidence. But the raven, keeper of secrets and carrier of messages, invites you to trust them. Grow quiet and curious; notice what keeps appearing. Some of those close, clever eyes upon you are not there to judge but to guard — and the message they carry is simply this: you are known, you are minded, and you are not walking this path alone.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns against reading fearful omens into everything, or ignoring a clear and helpful message because it is easier to call it chance.
affirm: I notice the signs, and I trust that I am watched over and never alone.

name: The Sphinx
artSubject: A serene, benevolent sphinx with the calm, beautiful, clearly visible face of a wise woman, warm knowing eyes and a gentle enigmatic smile, her tawny-gold lioness body resting regally on warm desert sandstone. Great feathered wings of turquoise and gold fold softly at her sides, and a delicate jeweled headdress catches the light. Behind her, a starlit desert sky glows with soft constellations and a low golden moon. Glowing hieroglyphs shimmer faintly on the stone, radiating ancient, kindly wisdom.
theme: The answers you seek already live within you; trust your own quiet wisdom.
message: The sphinx does not hand you the answer, beloved — she asks the riddle, because she knows the wisdom you are seeking is already sleeping inside you, waiting to be remembered. Grow quiet and listen past the noise; the deep, sure knowing you keep hunting for out there has been within you all along.
book: The Sphinx arrives when you are searching everywhere for an answer — asking everyone, reading everything — and still feel unsure. Her deeper teaching is that true wisdom is not given but uncovered; she speaks in riddles precisely so that you will stop grasping outside yourself and turn inward, where the knowing already lives. We often mistake wisdom for something we must collect from others, and grow anxious when no one hands us certainty. But the answers that matter most cannot be borrowed — they rise, quiet and clear, from your own depths the moment you grow still enough to hear them. Trust your inner voice. Sit with the question rather than fleeing it, honour the pause before the answer, and let your own insight surprise you. You are far wiser, beloved, than you have ever let yourself believe.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns of overthinking every riddle until clarity drowns in worry, or of doubting your own knowing so deeply that you hand your answers away to anyone but yourself.
affirm: I trust the quiet wisdom that already lives within me.

name: The Centaur
artSubject: A noble centaur with the warm, kind, clearly visible face of a wise guardian, gentle steady eyes and a calm confident smile, his powerful chestnut horse body strong and grounded. He holds a slender bow with a garland of leaves rather than an arrow, standing tall on a sunlit hilltop meadow. A cloak of forest green drapes his shoulders, wildflowers bloom at his hooves, and soft golden light haloes him. His balanced, dignified stance radiates strength married to gentle wisdom.
theme: When strength is joined with wisdom, you stand steady and sure in who you are.
message: The centaur is power and wisdom joined in a single body, beloved — the strong, sure legs of a horse and the thoughtful heart of a sage, neither one complete without the other. When your strength is guided by wisdom and your wisdom carried by strength, you become steady and unshaken, sure of exactly who you are.
book: The Centaur arrives when you feel pulled between force and thought — either charging ahead on raw strength alone or thinking so much that you cannot move at all. Its deeper teaching is that true steadiness is born where power and wisdom meet: the horse's mighty legs guided by the sage's clear mind, action and understanding woven into one whole being. We often imagine we must choose — to be strong or to be wise, bold or thoughtful — and feel torn in half by the choosing. But you were never meant to pick only one; your strength needs wisdom to aim it, and your wisdom needs strength to carry it into the world. Stand in both. Let your gentleness and your might work together, and you will find a grounded certainty no storm can topple — steady, sure, and wholly yourself.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns of strength that gallops ahead with no wisdom to guide it, or wisdom so cautious it never lets your true power stand up and move.
affirm: My strength and my wisdom stand together, and I am steady and sure in who I am.

name: The Golden Stag
artSubject: A graceful golden stag with a luminous shimmering coat, soft dark gentle eyes and magnificent antlers that branch like glowing tree limbs, each tine crowned with a tiny warm light. He stands serenely in a moonlit forest glade, a delicate garland of white flowers around his neck. Soft golden light streams between ancient trees, luminous mist curls low across the mossy ground, and fireflies drift like drifting stars. His calm, noble bearing radiates quiet reverence, gentleness and sacred grace.
theme: Move with grace and gentleness, and follow always what is sacred and true.
message: The Golden Stag steps softly through the forest, beloved, its antlers crowned with light, and it never once needs to rush or push to be seen. Move as it moves — gently, gracefully, with your heart pointed always toward what is sacred — and the whole wood will part to let you pass.
book: The Golden Stag appears when life is asking you to lead not with force but with grace, to walk your path so gently that you leave beauty rather than bruises behind you. Its deeper teaching is that true strength is quiet — the stag is powerful beyond measure, yet it moves through the world with a tenderness that harms nothing. We sometimes believe we must be loud or hard to be taken seriously, and mistake gentleness for weakness. But the Golden Stag follows only what is sacred and true, and its devotion is its power. Let yourself be devoted to something luminous — to kindness, to honesty, to love — and let that devotion set your pace. When you move with grace toward what is holy, you become something the world reveres without your ever having to demand it.
shadow: In shadow, this card cautions that gentleness can slip into timidity, or that devotion can wander toward what only glitters rather than what is genuinely sacred.
affirm: I move with gentle grace, devoted always to what is true.

name: The Giant
theme: The greater your strength, the gentler you can afford to be.
artSubject: A colossal yet kindly giant with a warm, weathered, smiling face, seated peacefully on a green hillside at golden hour, tenderly cradling a tiny glowing bird in one enormous cupped hand. Moss, ferns and wildflowers grow across his shoulders like a living landscape, soft clouds drift about his head, and a little village nestles safely in the valley below. Immense, gentle, reassuring and wondrous.
message: The giant could shake the mountains if he chose, beloved, yet look how carefully he cradles the smallest bird. Your strength was never meant to make others feel small — it shines brightest when it kneels down, softens its voice, and shelters what is tender.
book: The Giant arrives when you are learning the true measure of your own power — your size, your influence, the weight your words and choices carry for others. Its deeper teaching is that real strength is known not by how much it can crush but by how gently it can hold; the mightiest are those who choose, over and over, to be careful. We sometimes believe that being strong means being hard, or that being big means taking up all the room. The giant knows better — he steps lightly so as not to trample the flowers, and lifts the fallen without a moment's thought. Let your strength grow as vast as it likes, and let your gentleness grow vaster still. The world is not asking you to be smaller; it is asking you to be kind at your full height.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns of strength that forgets its own size and bruises without meaning to — or a gentle heart so frightened of its power that it hunches down and never rises to its full, protective height.
affirm: I am strong and I am gentle, and I use my strength to protect what is small.

name: The Genie
theme: The magic to grant your wish already rests in your own hands.
artSubject: A wondrous, friendly genie of swirling blue-and-violet smoke rising from an ornate golden lamp, with kind eyes and a warm inviting smile, arms open in offering. Glowing golden sparks and tiny stars spiral around, and within the drifting smoke faint dreamlike images shimmer — a castle, a rainbow, an open door. Rich jewel tones, magical and brimming with possibility.
message: The genie rises from the lamp with a knowing smile, beloved, but here is the secret it has always wanted you to learn: the wish-granting magic was never its alone. Name what you truly long for, out loud and unafraid — half of every wish is simply daring to want it.
book: The Genie appears when you have been shrinking your longing, asking for less than you dream of because wanting the whole of it feels greedy, or foolish, or impossible. Its deeper teaching is that possibility is far wider than fear allows you to believe, and that naming a wish clearly is the first real act of making it come true. We so often wait for some outside magic to rescue or reward us, forgetting that the genie grants only what is actually asked — and that the asking belongs to us. So dare to know what you want. Say it plainly, without apology. Then watch how the world begins, in ways both seen and unseen, to conspire with a heart brave enough to wish. The lamp was in your hands all along.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns against wishing your life away while never lifting a hand toward it, or chasing shiny wants that were never truly yours — and against believing the magic lives only somewhere outside of you.
affirm: I dare to name my truest wish, for the magic to begin it lives in me.

name: The Fairy Godmother
theme: Help is on its way — and yes, you are worthy of it.
artSubject: A warm, grandmotherly fairy godmother with a gentle radiant face and soft silver-white hair, wrapped in a shimmering pale-blue and gold gown, raising a glowing star-tipped wand that scatters trails of golden sparkles. Her kind eyes crinkle with love, soft light haloes her, and around her the ordinary turns wondrous — a pumpkin glinting gold, petals lifting into light. Tender, luminous and deeply reassuring.
message: Just when the night felt darkest, the fairy godmother steps softly into the room, beloved, with a wand full of light and no judgement in her eyes at all. Help is nearer than you think — and you do not have to earn it or deserve it perfectly. You are already worthy of good and gentle things.
book: The Fairy Godmother arrives in the hours when you feel forgotten and overlooked, certain that kindness and rescue are meant for other, luckier people. Its deeper teaching is that grace does not wait for you to be flawless; it comes precisely to the weary and the unnoticed, and it comes because you are loved — not because you have finally earned it. We so often refuse the very help we need, believing we must manage everything alone or prove our worth first. But the godmother never checks whether you deserve the gown; she simply lifts her wand because your heart is good and your need is real. Let yourself be helped. Say yes to the kindness that is arriving. You were always worthy of being cared for, exactly as you are tonight.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns against refusing every offered hand out of pride or a sense of unworthiness — or waiting so passively to be rescued that you forget the small, real magic in your own two hands.
affirm: I am worthy of kindness, and I let the help that is arriving reach me.

name: The Moon Hare
theme: In the stillness and the moonlight, quiet magic does its work.
artSubject: A gentle, enchanting hare with soft silver-grey fur sitting calmly in a moonlit meadow, gazing up at a huge luminous full moon in which its own silhouette is faintly mirrored. Dewy grass and glowing night-flowers, drifting fireflies and a scatter of stars, tender silver light everywhere. Peaceful, dreamlike and quietly magical.
message: The moon hare sits so still beneath the great bright moon, beloved, trusting the slow quiet magic of the night. Not everything must be chased or hurried — some of the most important things in your life are growing gently in the dark, while you rest. Be still a while, and let them.
book: The Moon Hare appears when you have been running hard, certain that if you stop, everything you are hoping for will slip away. Its deeper teaching is that stillness is not the same as giving up — some magic only ever works in the quiet, in the dark, in the resting, the way seeds swell unseen beneath the winter ground. We are taught that worth is measured in busyness, and so we are afraid to pause. But the hare beneath the moon knows a softer law: that gentleness and patience and rest are their own kind of power, doing their quiet work while the noisy world sleeps. Let yourself be still tonight. Trust what is unfolding out of sight. The moonlight is tending things on your behalf, and you do not have to earn the peace of simply being.
shadow: In shadow, this card warns against mistaking restlessness for progress, or fearing stillness so much that you never let the quiet magic finish its work.
affirm: I rest gently, and trust the quiet magic working on my behalf.
